text: The surgeon uses monopolar scissors to make an anterior gastrotomy approximately three to four centimeters in length on the stomach, assisted by intraoperative endoscopy to locate the incision site.

After creating the anterior gastrotomy, the surgeon locates the GIST tumor on the posterior wall of the stomach. A blue load stapler is used to resect the tumor, with the endoscope positioned just distal to the gastroesophageal junction. Two stapler loads are fired to complete the resection.

The surgeon uses TilePro to ensure the G-junction is not narrowed during the procedure. The gist tumor is placed in a catch bag. The anterior gastrotomy is closed in two layers using a NINI V-Lock suture. A repeat endoscopy confirms the G-junction remains unobstructed. The patient is discharged on post-operative day one without complications.
